content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 31,
1992
Shirley Wechsler
Campaign Finance Office
11783 Laureicrest Drive
Studio City,
CA
91604
RE:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92—114
Dear Ms. Wechsler:
You have requested advice on behalf of Senator David Roberti
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the 11
Act”) .1/
In your letter of February 28,
1992,
you have asked if the
funds transferred from Senator David Roberti’s regular campaign
account into his special election campaign account during the late
contribution reporting period,
March 22,
1991-April 6,
1992,
for
the April 7,
1992 election,
would trigger the requirement to file
late contribution reports.
The Commission has provided previous advice that the transfer
of funds from one committee controlled by a candidate to another
committee controlled by the same candidate during the late
contribution reporting period does not trigger the late
contribution filing requirement of Government Code Section 84203.
(Whitney Advice Letter,
No.
A—83-212,
copy enclosed.)
If you have any additional questions concerning this matter,
please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Enclosure
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 • .Sacrarnento CA 95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.question: If the funds transferred from Senator David Roberti's regular campaign account into his special election campaign account during the late contribution reporting period, March 22, 1991-April 6, 1992, for the April 7, 1992 election, would trigger the requirement to file late contribution reports.
sections.conclusion: The Commission has provided previous advice that the transfer of funds from one committee controlled by a candidate to another committee controlled by the same candidate during the late contribution reporting period does not trigger the late contribution filing requirement of Government Code Section 84203.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether transfers of campaign funds from one candidate-controlled committee to another candidate-controlled committee during the late contribution reporting period trigger late contribution filing requirements under Government Code Section 84203.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Transfers of funds between committees controlled by the same candidate do not trigger late contribution filing requirements, as established in prior FPPC advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document references prior Whitney Advice Letter No. A-83-212 for substantive precedent.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If the funds transferred from Senator David Roberti's regular campaign account into his special election campaign account during the late contribution reporting period, March 22, 1991-April 6, 1992, for the April 7, 1992 election, would trigger the requirement to file late contribution reports.

CONCLUSION: The Commission has provided previous advice that the transfer of funds from one committee controlled by a candidate to another committee controlled by the same candidate during the late contribution reporting period does not trigger the late contribution filing requirement of Government Code Section 84203.

The Commission has provided previous advice that the transfer of funds from one committee controlled by a candidate to another committee controlled by the same candidate during the late contribution reporting period does not trigger the late contribution filing requirement of Government Code Section 84203. (Whitney Advice Letter, No. A—83-212, copy enclosed.) If you have any additional questions concerning this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322—5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel Enclosure 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that transfers of campaign funds from Senator David Roberti's regular campaign account to his special election campaign account during the late contribution reporting period do not trigger late contribution reporting requirements under Government Code Section 84203.
